skill.name: lark-vc
skill.description: Search completed Lark video meetings and retrieve meeting notes, summaries, action items, chapters, and transcripts through lark-cli.
skill.summary: Find completed Lark video meetings and collect their notes, summaries, action items, and transcripts.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, network, and entropy patterns. Review found markdown instructions for lark-cli, not embedded executable code or prompt injection, but the skill can access sensitive meeting records and write downloaded artifacts locally. Publish with a clear warning about authenticated Lark access, external command execution, and local transcript files.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated External CLI Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run lark-cli commands for meeting search, document fetch, metadata lookup, and note retrieval. This is expected for the skill, but it executes external commands against an authenticated Lark account and can expose private meeting metadata if used outside the intended tenant context. Static command findings are true positives as operational risk, not evidence of malware.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files directly document lark-cli command execution with user authentication. The commands are legitimate Lark operations, so the risk is data exposure and command execution scope rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Meeting Content Retrieval
medium_findings.description: The skill guides agents to retrieve meeting notes, AI summaries, action items, chapters, and transcripts. These artifacts may contain confidential business discussions, participant data, and follow-up tasks, so usage requires least-privilege Lark scopes and user confirmation for broad searches or transcript retrieval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented purpose is to retrieve meeting notes and transcripts. This is core functionality, but the sensitivity of meeting artifacts makes the security impact clear.

medium_findings.title: Local Artifact Writes for Meeting Materials
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs downloads of cover whiteboards and transcript files into local artifact directories, and the notes shortcut supports an output directory and overwrite mode. This is legitimate, but local files may persist sensitive meeting content and should be written only to expected workspace paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write behavior is explicitly documented through output directories and downloaded transcript paths. No traversal exploit is shown, but persistence of sensitive files is a real operational risk.

low_findings.title: Static Filesystem and Entropy Findings Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported path traversal and entropy findings primarily come from markdown links, relative documentation paths, Chinese text, and code block syntax. No evidence found of encoded payloads, executable binaries, or path traversal logic in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows documentation references and ordinary markdown, not code that opens arbitrary paths. The high entropy signal is consistent with multilingual prose and dense command examples.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is an Example Input
low_findings.description: The network finding points to a sample Feishu minutes URL used to explain how to extract a minute token. No evidence found that the skill sends data to an unrelated external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in a parameter help table as an illustrative Feishu example. It is not a webhook, callback, or exfiltration endpoint.

dangerous_patterns.title: External lark-cli Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on shell-visible lark-cli commands to query Lark APIs and fetch documents. Users should run it only in a trusted workspace with an authenticated account intended for the requested meeting data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command usage is explicit and central to the skill. The pattern is expected, but still requires a trust warning because it acts through local credentials.

dangerous_patterns.title: Downloaded Meeting Artifacts
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can download whiteboard covers and transcripts to local directories. These files should be treated as sensitive and removed or protected according to organization policy.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly describes local downloads and transcript file paths. The risk is data persistence, not arbitrary filesystem compromise.

content.value_statement: Teams need fast access to completed Lark meeting records, notes, and transcripts. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to search meetings and retrieve the right meeting artifacts with lark-cli.
actual_capabilities: Search completed Lark video meetings by keyword, time range, organizer, participant, or meeting room.
Retrieve meeting note document tokens, transcript document tokens, and shared document tokens.
Fetch AI-generated summaries, action items, chapters, and transcript artifacts from Lark Minutes paths.
Guide pagination for meeting searches so large result sets are not missed.
Explain which Lark permissions are required for each meeting search or note retrieval path.
Route future meeting schedule questions to the Lark calendar skill instead of meeting record search.
limitations: It requires lark-cli, a logged-in Lark user account, and the correct Lark permission scopes.
It only searches completed video meetings, not future scheduled meetings.
It cannot access notes or transcripts when the authenticated user lacks meeting or minutes permissions.
Downloaded transcripts and cover images may remain in local artifact directories after use.

use_cases.title: Review a recent team meeting
use_cases.description: Search yesterday or this week for completed meetings, then open the correct note or transcript document.

use_cases.title: Collect action items after a project call
use_cases.description: Retrieve AI-generated todos and summaries from a Lark meeting note or minutes token.

use_cases.title: Audit meeting history by participant
use_cases.description: Filter historical video meetings by organizer, participant, room, and time range for review.

prompt_templates.title: Find recent meetings
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the completed Lark video meetings I attended yesterday and show the meeting titles, times, and available note links.

prompt_templates.title: Search by keyword
prompt_templates.prompt: Search completed Lark video meetings from last week with the keyword "roadmap" and include pagination if there are more results.

prompt_templates.title: Get meeting notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use these meeting IDs to retrieve Lark meeting notes. Show the summary document, transcript document, and shared documents separately.

prompt_templates.title: Extract action items from minutes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this Lark Minutes token to get the AI summary, todos, chapters, and transcript artifact. Keep downloaded files in one artifact directory.

output_examples.input: Find completed meetings this week about product launch.
output_examples.output: A concise list of matching completed Lark meetings with titles, times, organizers, meeting IDs, and whether notes are available.

output_examples.input: Get the notes for this meeting ID.
output_examples.output: Separate links or tokens for the smart note, transcript, and shared documents, with a short explanation of which one to use.

output_examples.input: Summarize action items from this minutes token.
output_examples.output: A meeting summary, grouped action items, chapter outline, and the local transcript artifact location when downloaded.

best_practices: Use the smallest time range or filter that can answer the meeting search request.
Confirm before fetching all pages when a search returns many meeting records.
Protect or delete local transcript artifacts after the user no longer needs them.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to search future meetings; use a Lark calendar skill instead.
Do not fetch full transcripts when a note link or summary is enough.
Do not use broad participant or room searches without a clear business purpose.

faq.question: Does this skill modify Lark meetings?
faq.answer: No. The documented commands search records and retrieve notes or artifacts. They do not edit meetings.

faq.question: Can it search upcoming meetings?
faq.answer: No. It is for completed video meetings. Use a Lark calendar workflow for future meetings.

faq.question: What credentials are required?
faq.answer: It requires lark-cli with a logged-in user account and the Lark scopes needed for the requested operation.

faq.question: Can it retrieve transcripts?
faq.answer: Yes, when the user has permission and the meeting has a transcript or minutes artifact.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill runs external lark-cli commands and can retrieve sensitive meeting content, but no malicious code was found.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when lark-cli is available.
